code: @Article{,   title = {marmap: A Package for Importing, Plotting and Analyzing Bathymetric and Topographic Data in R},   author = {Eric Pante and Benoit Simon-Bouhet},   year = {2013},   journal = {PLoS ONE},   volume = {8},   number = {9},   pages = {e73051},   note = {doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0073051}, }

what: Import, plot and analyse bathymetric data in R
title: Import, plot and analyse bathymetric data in R
text: marmap2 imports, converts plots bathymetric topographic data R. modernisation workspace historical marmap package, tidier function names, updated documentation, tibble-first workflows newer data import tools. NOAA ETOPO data can imported tibble-first approach:
code: library(marmap2) library(ggplot2)  dat <- get_gebco(   lon = c(2, 7),   lat = c(42, 44),   resolution = 0.5 )  dat |>   ggplot() +   geom_bathy(expand = FALSE) +   geom_coastline() +   geom_contour(     aes(x = lon, y = lat, z = depth),     color = "grey20",     breaks = seq(0, -2500, by = -200),     linewidth = 0.2   ) +   geom_contour(     aes(lon, lat, z = depth),     color = "grey20",     breaks = seq(0, 2500, by = 500),     linewidth = 0.2   ) +   scale_fill_bathy(     palette_ocean = "ocean_blues",     palette_land = "land_earth"   ) +   theme_bw() dat_noaa <- get_noaa(   lon1 = -6, lon2 = -5,   lat1 = 49, lat2 = 50,   resolution = 1 )

what: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
text: Converts three-column data frame containing longitude, latitude depth values matrix class bathy.

title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
code: as_bathy(x)

title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
text: x Three-column data frame longitude, latitude depth values.

title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
text: output as_bathy matrix class bathy, longitude stored row names latitude stored column names.

title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
text: first column interpreted longitude, second latitude, third depth elevation.

title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
text: Benoit Simon-Bouhet

title: Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class bathy — as_bathy
code: xyz <- data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),   depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140) )  bathy <- as_bathy(xyz) class(bathy) #> [1] "bathy" summary(bathy) #> Bathymetric data of class 'bathy', with 3 rows and 3 columns #> Latitudinal range: 48 to 50 (48 N to 50 N) #> Longitudinal range: -5 to -3 (5 W to 3 W) #> Cell size: 60 minute(s) #>  #> Depth statistics: #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    -160    -140    -110    -110     -80     -60  #>  #> First 5 columns and rows of the bathymetric matrix: #>      48   49   50 #> -5  -80  -70  -60 #> -4 -120 -110 -100 #> -3 -160 -150 -140

what: Convert bathymetric data to sf — as_sf
title: Convert bathymetric data to sf — as_sf
text: Converts object class bathy, long table containing longitude latitude columns, sf object point geometries.

title: Convert bathymetric data to sf — as_sf
code: as_sf(x, lon = "lon", lat = "lat", crs = 4326, remove = FALSE, ...)

title: Convert bathymetric data to sf — as_sf
text: x object class bathy, data frame/tibble containing longitude latitude columns. lon, lat Character. Names longitude latitude columns used x data frame tibble. crs Coordinate reference system assigned returned sf object. Defaults 4326, .e. WGS84 longitude/latitude. remove Logical. TRUE, coordinate columns removed returned attribute table. Defaults FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed sf::st_as_sf.

title: Convert bathymetric data to sf — as_sf
text: object class sf point geometries.

title: Convert bathymetric data to sf — as_sf
code: xyz <- data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),   depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140) )  xyz_sf <- as_sf(xyz) class(xyz_sf) #> [1] "sf"         "data.frame"

what: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
title: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
text: Converts bathymetric data stored long table historical bathy matrix modern terra::SpatRaster.

title: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
code: as_spatraster(x, crs = "EPSG:4326", lon = "lon", lat = "lat", depth = "depth")

title: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
text: x data frame/tibble longitude, latitude, depth columns, object inheriting class bathy. crs Coordinate reference system assigned returned raster. Can CRS string "EPSG:4326", numeric EPSG code 4326. Defaults "EPSG:4326". lon, lat, depth Column names used x data frame/tibble.

title: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
text: terra::SpatRaster object.

title: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
text: tabular input, x must represent complete regular grid. first two coordinate columns interpreted cell centres. bathy input, row names interpreted longitude column names latitude.

title: Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster — as_spatraster
code: if (requireNamespace("terra", quietly = TRUE)) {   xyz <- data.frame(     lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),     lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),     depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140)   )    r <- as_spatraster(xyz) }

title: Convert between bathy objects and tibbles — bathy_to_tbl
text: bathy_to_tbl() converts historical matrix-based bathy class long tibble longitude, latitude, depth/elevation columns. tbl_to_bathy() converts long table back bathy object.

what: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
text: Collates two bathy matrices, one longitude 0 180 degrees East, longitude 0 180 degrees West

title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
code: collate_bathy(east,west)

title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
text: east matrix class bathy eastern data (West antimeridian) west matrix class bathy western data (East antimeridian)

title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
text: single matrix class bathy. plotting collated data geom_bathy, use antimeridian = TRUE display western longitude labels beyond 180 degrees.

title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
text: function used internally import functions data downloaded sides antimeridian line (180 degrees longitude). , example, data downloaded longitudes 170E-180 180-170W, collate_bathy() creates single matrix class bathy coordinate system going 170 190 degrees longitude. get_noaa() deals data sides antimeridian need processing collate_bathy().

title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
text: Eric Pante

title: Collates two bathy matrices with data from either sides of the antimeridian — collate_bathy
code: east <- as_bathy(data.frame(   lon = rep(c(178, 179), each = 2),   lat = rep(c(10, 11), times = 2),   depth = c(-10, -20, -30, -40) )) west <- as_bathy(data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-180, -179), each = 2),   lat = rep(c(10, 11), times = 2),   depth = c(-50, -60, -70, -80) ))  collate_bathy(east, west) #>      10  11 #> 178 -10 -20 #> 179 -30 -40 #> 180 -50 -60 #> 181 -70 -80 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "bathy"

what: Coordinate system for sf data around the antimeridian — coord_sf_antimeridian
title: Coordinate system for sf data around the antimeridian — coord_sf_antimeridian
text: small wrapper around ggplot2::coord_sf keeps standard coord_sf() behaviour allows longitude axis labels beyond 180 degrees displayed western longitudes.

title: Coordinate system for sf data around the antimeridian — coord_sf_antimeridian
code: coord_sf_antimeridian(   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   expand = TRUE,   crs = NULL,   default_crs = NULL,   datum = sf::st_crs(4326),   label_graticule = ggplot2::waiver(),   label_axes = ggplot2::waiver(),   lims_method = "cross",   ndiscr = 100,   default = FALSE,   clip = "on",   reverse = "none",   x_breaks = ggplot2::waiver() )

title: Coordinate system for sf data around the antimeridian — coord_sf_antimeridian
text: xlim, ylim Limits x y axes. limits specified units default CRS. default, means projected coordinates (default_crs = NULL). limit specifications translate exact region shown plot can confusing non-linear rotated coordinate systems used default crs. First, different methods can preferable different conditions. See parameter lims_method details. Second, specifying limits along one direction can affect automatically generated limits along direction. Therefore, best always specify limits x y. Third, specifying limits via position scales xlim()/ylim() strongly discouraged, can result data points dropped plot even though visible final plot region. expand TRUE, default, adds small expansion factor limits ensure data axes overlap. FALSE, limits taken exactly data xlim/ylim. Giving logical vector separately control expansion four directions (top, left, bottom right). expand argument recycled length 4 necessary. Alternatively, can named logical vector control single direction, e.g. expand = c(bottom = FALSE). crs coordinate reference system (CRS) data projected plotting. specified, use CRS defined first sf layer plot. default_crs default CRS used non-sf layers (carry CRS information) scale limits. default value NULL means setting crs used. implies non-sf layers scale limits assumed specified projected coordinates. useful alternative setting default_crs = sf::st_crs(4326), means x y positions interpreted longitude latitude, respectively, World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS84). datum CRS provides datum use generating graticules. label_graticule Character vector indicating graticule lines labeled . Meridians run north-south, letters "N" "S" indicate labeled north south end points, respectively. Parallels run east-west, letters "E" "W" indicate labeled east west end points, respectively. Thus, label_graticule = "SW" label meridians south end parallels west end, whereas label_graticule = "EW" label parallels ends meridians . meridians parallels can general intersect side plot panel, choice label_graticule labels guaranteed reside one particular side plot panel. Also, label_graticule can cause labeling artifacts, particular graticule line coincides edge plot panel. circumstances, label_axes generally yield better results used instead. parameter can used alone combination label_axes. label_axes Character vector named list character values specifying graticule lines (meridians parallels) labeled side plot. Meridians indicated "E" (East) parallels "N" (North). Default "--EN", specifies (clockwise top) labels top, none right, meridians bottom, parallels left. Alternatively, setting specified list(bottom = "E", left = "N"). parameter can used alone combination label_graticule. lims_method Method specifying scale limits converted limits plot region. effect default_crs = NULL. non-linear CRS (e.g., perspective centered around North pole), available methods yield widely differing results, may want try various options. Methods currently implemented include "cross" (default), "box", "orthogonal", "geometry_bbox". method "cross", limits along one direction (e.g., longitude) applied midpoint direction (e.g., latitude). method avoids excessively large limits rotated coordinate systems means sometimes limits need expanded little extreme data points included final plot region. contrast, method "box", box generated limits along directions, limits projected coordinates chosen entire box visible. method can yield plot regions large. Finally, method "orthogonal" applies limits separately along axis, method "geometry_bbox" ignores limit information except bounding boxes objects geometry aesthetic. ndiscr Number segments use discretising graticule lines; try increasing number graticules look incorrect. default default coordinate system? FALSE (default), replacing coordinate system another one creates message alerting user coordinate system replaced. TRUE, warning suppressed. clip drawing clipped extent plot panel? setting "" (default) means yes, setting "" means . cases, default "" changed, setting clip = "" can cause unexpected results. allows drawing data points anywhere plot, including plot margins. limits set via xlim ylim data points fall outside limits, data points may show places axes, legend, plot title, plot margins. reverse string giving directions reverse. "none" (default) keeps directions . "x" "y" can used reverse respective directions. "xy" can used reverse directions. x_breaks Breaks used x axis. default lets ggplot2 choose breaks automatically.

title: Coordinate system for sf data around the antimeridian — coord_sf_antimeridian
text: ggplot2 coordinate system.

what: Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf coordinates — geom_bathy
title: Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf coordinates — geom_bathy
text: Adds raster-like bathymetry layer long table, point sf object, bathy object. data kept regular x/y/z data, ggplot2 layers ggplot2::geom_contour can added plot. ggplot2::coord_sf coordinate system added automatically, coord_sf_antimeridian antimeridian = TRUE. Projected tibbles returned project_bathy detected automatically plotted ggplot2::coord_sf using projected CRS.

title: Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf coordinates — geom_bathy
code: geom_bathy(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   ...,   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   depth = "depth",   geom = c("tile", "raster"),   crs = 4326,   antimeridian = FALSE,   coord = c("auto", "sf", "fixed"),   asp = 1,   x_breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   expand = TRUE,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf coordinates — geom_bathy
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes. x, y, fill missing, default lon, lat, depth. data long data frame/tibble, point sf object, bathy object. NULL, data inherited ggplot2::ggplot. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_tile ggplot2::geom_raster. geom = "tile", cell borders coloured like fill default mask anti-aliasing seams adjacent tiles. lon, lat, depth Character. Names longitude, latitude, depth columns. geom Character. Rendering geom, either "tile" "raster". "tile" default works quietly ggplot2::coord_sf. coord_sf(), ggplot2 draws geom_raster() rectangle fallback may emit informational message. crs Coordinate reference system used ggplot2::coord_sf non-sf layers. Defaults 4326, .e. WGS84 longitude/latitude. antimeridian Logical. TRUE, uses longitude labels adapted data crossing antimeridian. coord Character. Coordinate system add. "auto" default uses "sf" geographic projected bathymetry. Projected tibbles returned project_bathy detected automatically destination CRS used. "sf" uses ggplot2::coord_sf coord_sf_antimeridian. "fixed" uses ggplot2::coord_fixed. asp Numeric. Aspect ratio used coord = "fixed". default 1 gives longitude latitude degrees graphical scale. x_breaks Breaks used x axis antimeridian = TRUE. default lets ggplot2 choose breaks automatically. expand Logical character vector passed ggplot2::coord_sf. Use expand = FALSE remove padding around bathymetric grid keeping geographic axis labels. na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes Arguments passed selected geom.

title: Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf coordinates — geom_bathy
text: list ggplot2 components can added plot +.

title: Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf coordinates — geom_bathy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  xyz <- data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),   depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140) )  xyz |>   ggplot() +   geom_bathy() +   geom_contour(aes(lon, lat, z = depth), color = "white") } # }

what: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
text: Downloads user-defined geographic subset official GEBCO download service returns tibble object class bathy.

title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
code: get_gebco(   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   lon1 = NULL,   lon2 = NULL,   lat1 = NULL,   lat2 = NULL,   resolution = 1,   antimeridian = FALSE,   keep = FALSE,   path = NULL,   class = c("tbl", "bathy") )

title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
text: lon Numeric vector length 2 giving longitude bounds decimal degrees. recommended syntax. lat Numeric vector length 2 giving latitude bounds decimal degrees. recommended syntax. lon1 First longitude bound decimal degrees. Alternative lon. lon2 Second longitude bound decimal degrees. Alternative lon. lat1 First latitude bound decimal degrees. Alternative lat. lat2 Second latitude bound decimal degrees. Alternative lat. resolution Output grid spacing arc-minutes. Defaults 1. GEBCO global NetCDF subset downloaded native 15 arc-second resolution, resampled locally nearest-neighbour selection resolution > 0.25. Values lower 0.25 return native GEBCO global grid resolution. antimeridian Logical. TRUE, requested longitudinal range interpreted crossing antimeridian. function downloads two GEBCO subsets stitches one bathy object. order lon1 lon2 matter. keep Logical. TRUE, xyz table returned bathy object also written csv file path. path Directory used cached csv files keep = TRUE, get_gebco looks already downloaded matching csv file. Defaults current working directory. class Character. Class returned object. Use "tbl" (default) return tibble columns lon, lat, depth; use "bathy" return historical matrix class bathy.

title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
text: tibble default, object class bathy class = "bathy". keep = TRUE, csv copy downloaded xyz table written path.

title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
text: get_gebco uses workflow implemented official GEBCO Grid Subsetting App https://download.gebco.net/. function submits small JSON basket /api/queue, polls /api/queue/status/{basketId} basket ready, downloads resulting zip archive /api/queue/download/{basketId}, extracts NetCDF file, reads lat, lon, elevation variables, converts requested output class. current global GEBCO grids served GEBCO download service native resolution 15 arc-seconds, .e. 0.25 arc-minutes 0.0041667 decimal degrees. corresponds approximately 463 m equator. resolution argument expressed arc-minutes consistency get_noaa, sent GEBCO API: NetCDF file downloaded native resolution, resampled locally nearest-neighbour selection regular grid resolution larger 0.25. Values smaller 0.25 therefore increase spatial resolution return native grid. GEBCO also exposes higher-resolution regional experimental products download service, example polar grids beta multi-resolution layers. function intentionally targets default global longitude/latitude bathymetry layer whose NetCDF variables named lon, lat, elevation. GEBCO download service expects left <= right; areas crossing antimeridian therefore downloaded two geographic subsets, one eastern longitude bound 180 degrees one -180 degrees western longitude bound. two subsets stitched single bathy object whose longitudes expressed 0-360 degree range. get_noaa, order lon1/lon2 lat1/lat2 matter: function sorts coordinate bounds internally submitting requests GEBCO.

title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
text: GEBCO Compilation Group. GEBCO Grid, continuous terrain model oceans land 15 arc-second intervals. https://www.gebco.net/

title: Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service — get_gebco
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download a one-degree subset from the official GEBCO service b <- get_gebco(   lon = c(-6, -5),   lat = c(49, 50),   resolution = 1 )  } # }

what: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
text: Imports bathymetric topographic data NOAA ETOPO 2022 image service, given coordinate bounds requested spatial resolution.

title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
code: get_noaa(   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   lon1 = NULL,   lon2 = NULL,   lat1 = NULL,   lat2 = NULL,   resolution = 4,   antimeridian = FALSE,   keep = FALSE,   path = NULL,   class = c("tbl", "bathy") )

title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
text: lon Numeric vector length 2 giving longitude bounds decimal degrees. recommended syntax. lat Numeric vector length 2 giving latitude bounds decimal degrees. recommended syntax. lon1 First longitude bound area bathymetric data downloaded, decimal degrees. Alternative lon. lon2 Second longitude bound area bathymetric data downloaded, decimal degrees. Alternative lon. lat1 First latitude bound area bathymetric data downloaded, decimal degrees. Alternative lat. lat2 Second latitude bound area bathymetric data downloaded, decimal degrees. Alternative lat. resolution Requested grid resolution arc-minutes. Defaults 4. antimeridian Logical. Whether requested region crosses antimeridian, longitude 180 -180. keep Logical. Whether write downloaded xyz table disk. Defaults FALSE. path Directory used cached csv files keep = TRUE, get_noaa() looks already downloaded matching data. Defaults current working directory. class Character. Class returned object. Use "tbl" (default) return tibble columns lon, lat, depth; use "bathy" return historical matrix class bathy.

title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
text: tibble default, object class bathy class = "bathy". keep = TRUE, csv file containing downloaded xyz table written path. file named using format marmap_coord_COORDINATES_res_RESOLUTION.csv, coordinates separated semicolons; antimeridian requests add _anti suffix.

title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
text: get_noaa() queries ETOPO 2022 database hosted NOAA, using coordinates area interest desired resolution. function uses NOAA ArcGIS image service returns long tibble default, matrix class bathy class = "bathy". resolution argument expressed arc-minutes. function uses 15 arc-second ETOPO 2022 layer resolution = 0.25, 30 arc-second layer resolution = 0.5, 60 arc-second layer coarser resolutions. Values lower 0.5 rounded 0.25; values 0.5 1 rounded 0.5. Users can optionally write downloaded data disk keep = TRUE. identical query performed later, using longitudes, latitudes, resolution, antimeridian setting, get_noaa() load local file instead querying NOAA server . behaviour used preferentially reduce unnecessary queries NOAA service reduce data loading time. several identical queries forced download fresh data, cached csv file must renamed, removed, moved outside path. get_noaa() can download bathymetric data around antimeridian antimeridian = TRUE. antimeridian 180th meridian, located Pacific Ocean, east New Zealand Fiji west Hawaii Tonga. pair longitude values -150 150, two different areas can requested: 60 degree-wide area centered antimeridian antimeridian = TRUE, 300 degree-wide area centered prime meridian antimeridian = FALSE. Data around antimeridian require two distinct NOAA queries, keep = TRUE can especially useful case. order longitude latitude bounds matter: get_noaa() sorts coordinate bounds internally querying NOAA. Longitude latitude bounds preferably supplied vector syntax lon = c(lon1, lon2) lat = c(lat1, lat2). explicit lon1, lon2, lat1, lat2 arguments remain available compatibility older code.

title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
text: NOAA National Centers Environmental Information. 2022: ETOPO 2022 15 Arc-Second Global Relief Model. NOAA National Centers Environmental Information. doi:10.25921/fd45-gt74

title: Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022 — get_noaa
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Query NOAA ETOPO 2022 for the North Atlantic at 10 arc-minutes. atl <- get_noaa(   lon = c(-20, -90),   lat = c(50, 20),   resolution = 10 )  # Same query using explicit lon1/lon2/lat1/lat2 arguments. atl_tbl <- get_noaa(   lon1 = -20, lon2 = -90,   lat1 = 50, lat2 = 20,   resolution = 10 )  # Download speed for a 10 x 10 degree area at 30 arc-minutes. system.time(get_noaa(lon = c(0, 10), lat = c(0, 10), resolution = 30))  # Antimeridian request around the Aleutian Islands. aleu <- get_noaa(   lon = c(165, -145),   lat = c(50, 65),   resolution = 5,   antimeridian = TRUE ) } # }

title: Test whether an object is of class bathy — is_bathy

title: Test whether an object is of class bathy — is_bathy
text: Eric Pante

title: Test whether an object is of class bathy — is_bathy
code: xyz <- data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),   depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140) )  is_bathy(xyz) #> [1] FALSE is_bathy(as_bathy(xyz)) #> [1] TRUE

what: Import, plot and analyze bathymetric and topographic data — marmap2-package
title: Import, plot and analyze bathymetric and topographic data — marmap2-package
text: marmap2 experimental modernization marmap, package designed downloading, converting plotting bathymetric topographic data R. development version currently focuses compact modern core: NOAA/GEBCO import, tibble-based workflows, conversion helpers, sf/terra interoperability, ggplot2-based bathymetric maps.

title: Import, plot and analyze bathymetric and topographic data — marmap2-package

title: Project bathymetric grids — project_bathy
text: Projects bathymetric data destination coordinate reference system returns tibble suitable plotting geom_bathy.

title: Project bathymetric grids — project_bathy
code: project_bathy(   x,   crs_to,   crs_from = "EPSG:4326",   resolution = NULL,   method = "bilinear",   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   depth = "depth",   names = c("lon", "lat", "depth"),   na.rm = TRUE )

title: Project bathymetric grids — project_bathy
text: x data frame/tibble longitude, latitude, depth columns, object inheriting class bathy. crs_to Destination coordinate reference system. Can CRS string "EPSG:3857", numeric EPSG code 3857. Passed terra::project. crs_from Source coordinate reference system. Defaults "EPSG:4326". Can also supplied numeric EPSG code 4326. resolution Optional output resolution destination map units. NULL, terra chooses suitable resolution. method Resampling method passed terra::project. Use "bilinear" continuous bathymetry/elevation values "near" nearest neighbour resampling. lon, lat, depth Column names used x data frame/tibble. names Character vector length 3 giving names projected coordinate depth columns returned tibble. Defaults c("lon", "lat", "depth"). na.rm Logical. TRUE, cells missing depth values removed returned tibble.

title: Project bathymetric grids — project_bathy
text: tibble projected coordinates depth values. source CRS, destination CRS, projected terra::SpatRaster stored attributes named crs_from, crs_to, spatraster.

title: Project bathymetric grids — project_bathy
text: Projection regular longitude/latitude bathymetric grid generally requires resampling. project_bathy() therefore converts input terra::SpatRaster, projects raster terra::project(), converts projected grid back long tibble. output bathy object projected coordinates longitude/latitude row column names. plot projected result geom_bathy(), use coord = "fixed" returned lon lat columns contain projected coordinates, geographic longitude/latitude values:
code: dat_proj |>   ggplot2::ggplot() +   geom_bathy(     ggplot2::aes(lon, lat, fill = depth),     coord = "fixed"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Easting", y = "Northing")

text: Reads three-column table containing longitude (x), latitude (y) depth (z) data.

title: Read bathymetric data in XYZ format — read_bathy
code: read_bathy(xyz, header = FALSE, sep = ",", ...)

title: Read bathymetric data in XYZ format — read_bathy
text: xyz three-column table longitude (x), latitude (y) depth (z) (default) header whether table row column names (default = FALSE) sep character separating columns, (default=",") ... arguments passed read.table()

title: Read bathymetric data in XYZ format — read_bathy
text: output read_bathy matrix class bathy. dimensions depend resolution extent input xyz table.

title: Read bathymetric data in XYZ format — read_bathy
text: Eric Pante

title: Read bathymetric data in XYZ format — read_bathy
code: xyz <- data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),   depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140) )  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.table(xyz, tmp, sep = ",", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE) bathy <- read_bathy(tmp, header = TRUE) class(bathy) #> [1] "bathy"

what: Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2 — scale_fill_bathy
title: Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2 — scale_fill_bathy
text: scale_fill_bathy() provides perceptually ordered colour scales bathymetric topographic rasters drawn ggplot2. ocean land parts scale selected independently: use palette name map side colour gradient; use single colour name code draw side constant colour; use NULL leave side undefined; use palette_ocean palette_land map depths altitudes separate palettes joined sea level.

title: Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2 — scale_fill_bathy
code: scale_fill_bathy(   palette_ocean = "ocean_blues",   palette_land = "land_earth",   limits = NULL,   mode = c("rescale", "truncate"),   na.value = "grey90",   name = "depth",   oob = NULL,   ... )  bathy_palette(palette = "ocean_blues", n = 256)  bathy_palettes(type = c("all", "ocean", "land"))

title: Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2 — scale_fill_bathy
text: palette_ocean, palette_land Palette definition used ocean values (<= 0) land values (>= 0), respectively. Can palette name, single colour, vector colours, function taking n returning colours, NULL. limits Numeric vector length two. Limits fill scale. NULL, scale limits trained plotted data. mode Character. Either "rescale" "truncate". "truncate", legend spans data range colours remain anchored fixed bathymetric/topographic reference depths, depth altitude keeps colour across maps. "rescale", selected palette part stretched plotted data range. na.value Colour used missing values. name Scale name passed ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn. oob Function used --bounds values. NULL, values squished nearest scale limit. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn. palette Palette name, single colour, colour vector, function taking n returning colours. n Integer. Number colours returned bathy_palette(). type Character. Palette family list. One "", "ocean", "land".

title: Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2 — scale_fill_bathy
text: ggplot2 fill scale. bathy_palette(), character vector colours. bathy_palettes(), character vector palette names.

title: Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2 — scale_fill_bathy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dat |>   ggplot2::ggplot() +   geom_bathy(ggplot2::aes(lon, lat, fill = depth), expand = FALSE) +   scale_fill_bathy(palette_land = "grey85")  dat |>   ggplot2::ggplot() +   geom_bathy(ggplot2::aes(lon, lat, fill = depth), expand = FALSE) +   scale_fill_bathy(     palette_ocean = "ocean_blues",     palette_land = "land_hcl"   )  dat |>   ggplot2::ggplot() +   geom_bathy(ggplot2::aes(lon, lat, fill = depth), expand = FALSE) +   scale_fill_bathy(palette_ocean = "#D8EEF3", palette_land = "land_hcl")  dat |>   ggplot2::ggplot() +   geom_bathy(ggplot2::aes(lon, lat, fill = depth), expand = FALSE) +   scale_fill_bathy(     palette_ocean = grDevices::hcl.colors(256, "Blues 3"),     palette_land = function(n) grDevices::hcl.colors(n, "Terrain 2")   ) } # }

what: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
title: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
text: Summary bathymetric data class bathy. Provides geographic bounds resolution (minutes) dataset, statistics depth data, preview bathymetric matrix.

title: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
code: summary_bathy(object, ...)

title: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
text: object object class bathy ... additional arguments affecting summary produced (see base function summary).

title: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
text: Information geographic bounds dataset (minimum maximum latitude longitude), resolution matrix minutes, statistics depth data (e.g. min, max, median...), preview data.

title: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
text: Eric Pante Benoit Simon-Bouhet

title: Summary of bathymetric data of class bathy — summary_bathy
code: xyz <- data.frame(   lon = rep(c(-5, -4, -3), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(48, 49, 50), times = 3),   depth = c(-80, -70, -60, -120, -110, -100, -160, -150, -140) )  bathy <- as_bathy(xyz) summary(bathy) #> Bathymetric data of class 'bathy', with 3 rows and 3 columns #> Latitudinal range: 48 to 50 (48 N to 50 N) #> Longitudinal range: -5 to -3 (5 W to 3 W) #> Cell size: 60 minute(s) #>  #> Depth statistics: #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    -160    -140    -110    -110     -80     -60  #>  #> First 5 columns and rows of the bathymetric matrix: #>      48   49   50 #> -5  -80  -70  -60 #> -4 -120 -110 -100 #> -3 -160 -150 -140
